_README: Operator-local linked-repo registry for loom's linkage system.
Resolved through .claude/bin/lib/loom-links.mjs (the single source
of truth every linkage-aware loom tool reads from).
PURPOSE
loom coordinates across sibling repos (BUILD repos, USE templates,
the loom self-checkout, atelier, downstream consumers). It used to
resolve them POSITIONALLY (~/repos/<name>). That positional guess
is the bug being removed: it breaks on any non-default layout and
re-creates the issue #255 / #252 disclosure class if a registry is
embedded inline in a synced artifact. The registry now lives ONLY
in the gitignored local copy of this file.
COPY INSTRUCTION
cp .claude/bin/loom-links.local.example.json \
.claude/bin/loom-links.local.json
$EDITOR .claude/bin/loom-links.local.json
GITIGNORED + NEVER SYNCED
loom-links.local.json is gitignored and is NEVER committed or
synced. Only THIS .example schema (synthetic tokens only) is
committed and reaches downstream repos. Do not put a real path,
org slug, or hostname in this committed template.
OVERRIDE
$LOOM_LINKS_CONFIG (an ABSOLUTE path) takes precedence over the
local file. Precedence: $LOOM_LINKS_CONFIG > loom-links.local.json
> fail-loud (no silent positional fallback, ever).
KEY VOCABULARY (canonical logical identities)
build.py / build.rs / build.prism            BUILD repos
use-template.py / .rs / .rb                  USE templates
use-template.claude-py / .claude-rs / .claude-rb
legacy CLI templates
loom                                         the loom self-checkout
atelier                                      CC + CO authority repo
downstream.<slug>                            any downstream consumer
Arbitrary downstream.* keys are accepted. Keys beginning with `_`
(like this _README) are ignored by the loader (forward-compat).
reposRoot / ~ SEMANTICS
reposRoot is the base directory holding all repo checkouts. A
leading `~` expands to $HOME. Each string entry (and each path
entry) is resolved RELATIVE to reposRoot. Default if omitted:
`~/repos`.
PATH OR URL
A links entry is EITHER a string (a path relative to reposRoot,
repin-compatible) OR an object { path?, url?, absolute? }:
path      relative to reposRoot (verbatim if absolute:true)
absolute  when true, `path` is used verbatim
url       a git URL, used verbatim (for repos not on disk)
Setting BOTH path and url on one entry is rejected (ambiguous).
FORWARD-COMPAT
Unknown keys are accepted; `_`-prefixed keys are ignored. The
loader validates SHAPE, not key membership.
SHARDS (repin-compat)
The optional `shards` block maps a shard label -> array of repo
paths relative to reposRoot. repin-downstream.mjs reads this via
loom-links.mjs::resolveShard once you migrate off the legacy
repin-targets.local.json file.
